versionIncrementPolicy: This record follows the same patch-versus-minor distinction already established elsewhere in this project's own governance versioning (e.g. ENGLISH_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json / ARABIC_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json's baselineVersion 1.0.0 -> 1.0.1 -> 1.0.2 pattern): a minor version (x.Y.0) is used when new scope or a new authorized action is granted; a patch version (x.y.Z) is used when an already-granted section is corrected, clarified, or formally ratified without adding new scope. 1.3.1 is a patch, not a minor bump, because it corrects defects in the 1.3.0 grant (packaging model, terminology, glossary-enforcement rule) without authorizing anything beyond what 1.3.0 already intended to authorize. 1.3.2 is likewise a patch, not a minor bump: it records the explicit project-owner ratification of the already-corrected 1.3.1 scope, without changing that scope in any way. No technical scope, output path, restriction, or dependency changes at 1.3.2 — only the governance status changes from 'technically corrected but not yet explicitly ratified by the project owner' to 'explicitly ratified by the project owner.'
versionNote: 1.0.0 authorized draft-form canonical content generation only, for the same four topics, and explicitly did not authorize any application build, packaging, or deployment. 1.1.0 is a controlled, additive amendment that authorizes only a local, internal Chapter 1 MVP application build under apps/chapter1-mvp/, for the same four already-authorized topics. It does not expand topic scope, does not authorize publication or deployment, and does not change any content or governance record's approval status. 1.2.0 is a further controlled, additive amendment that authorizes English-language canonical DRAFTING only (not approval of a baseline, not application build, not publication) for exactly two additional topics — ch01-t01 and ch01-t04 (Batch 1) — following completion of their scientific-reconciliation prerequisite (ch01-corr-001 and ch01-corr-002, both editoriallyApproved in SCIENTIFIC_CORRECTIONS.json). It does not expand scope.authorizedTopicIds or applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds below, both of which remain exactly the original four topics, and does not authorize Arabic content, visual production, application expansion, or student-facing publication for ch01-t01 or ch01-t04. 1.3.0 was a further controlled, additive amendment intended to authorize Arabic candidate-draft generation only (not Arabic baseline approval, not visual production, not application expansion, not publication) for the same two Batch 1 topics — ch01-t01 and ch01-t04 — following completion of the approved English baseline (ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.0) and of all nine Batch 1 glossary actions (BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json v1.3.0, all blocking terms approved). **1.3.0 as originally written contained three material scope defects, corrected in 1.3.1 below: (a) it authorized in-place editing of the approved English baseline files instead of separate candidate copies; (b) it mislabeled the authorized work as "Arabic canonical generation" rather than "Arabic candidate-draft generation," risking confusion with canonical/baseline status; (c) it prohibited use of any Arabic terminology outside the nine Batch 1 glossary records, rather than enforcing those nine where applicable while permitting ordinary Arabic language and other already-approved glossary terms.** 1.3.1 is a corrective patch, not a new grant of scope: it replaces the defective packaging model, terminology, and glossary-enforcement rule in batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization below with a corrected version, without expanding scope.authorizedTopicIds, applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds, or batch1DraftingAuthorization.applicableTopicIds above, all of which remain unchanged, and without authorizing anything 1.3.0 did not already intend to authorize. 1.3.2 is the explicit project-owner ratification of the corrected 1.3.1 scope. It is important governance history that 1.3.0 was created before the project owner had issued the intended explicit authorization decision, and that 1.3.1 was a technical-correction task rather than that explicit decision; 1.3.2 supplies the explicit project-owner ratification itself. 1.3.2 changes no technical scope, output path, restriction, or dependency established at 1.3.1 — it records the project owner's explicit authorization decision and nothing more. No Arabic content was generated and no candidate directory existed before this ratification. This version does not itself generate any Arabic content. See batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization (including its projectOwnerRatification block), authorizationHistory, docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_AUTHORIZATION_CORRECTION_RECORD.md, and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_GENERATION_RATIFICATION_RECORD.md below. 1.4.0 is a further controlled, additive amendment that authorizes original, rights-safe VISUAL PRODUCTION only (not application integration, not student-facing authorization, not deployment or publication) for exactly two visual IDs — ch01-t01-visual-001 and ch01-t04-visual-001 — following completion of the approved English Batch 1 baseline (ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.0) and the approved Arabic Batch 1 baseline (ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.0). It does not expand scope.authorizedTopicIds or applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds above, both of which remain exactly the original four topics. This version does not itself create any visual asset. See batch1VisualProductionAuthorization and authorizationHistory below. 1.5.0 is a further controlled, additive amendment that authorizes INTERNAL APPLICATION INTEGRATION only (not student-facing release, not public deployment, not publication) for the same two Batch 1 topics — ch01-t01 and ch01-t04 — following completion of the approved English Batch 1 baseline (v1.0.0), the approved Arabic Batch 1 baseline (v1.0.0), the approved Visual Batch 1 baseline (v1.0.0), and the registration of all 18 required Batch 1 identifiers (IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json v1.3.0). It does not expand scope.authorizedTopicIds or applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds above, both of which remain exactly the original four topics — ch01-t01 and ch01-t04 gain their own separate, narrower application-integration authorization instead, mirroring how each earlier Batch 1 milestone was recorded in its own dedicated section rather than by modifying the original four-topic grants. This version authorizes a future integration task; it does not itself integrate either topic, add any route, import, or registry entry, modify any application file, or run any build/test/install/deploy command. See batch1ApplicationIntegrationAuthorization and authorizationHistory below. 1.6.0 is a further controlled, additive amendment that authorizes PUBLIC DEPLOYMENT HOSTING of the apps/chapter1-mvp/ application, as currently application-integration-authorized, to GitHub Pages via a GitHub Actions workflow, at the explicit request and explicit authorization of the project owner (khubrani2a-gif) given directly in chat on 2026-07-17. This is a narrow authorization of the *hosting mechanism* only — it makes the already-built, already-governance-labeled application reachable at a public URL (https://khubrani2a-gif.github.io/PHSH111-/) for review/demonstration purposes. It explicitly does NOT authorize setting studentFacingAllowed or studentPublicationAuthorized to true for any topic or record, does NOT constitute pedagogical student release or content publication in the sense used elsewhere in this record, does NOT authorize any content, governance-flag, or application-behavior change, and does NOT expand scope.authorizedTopicIds or applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds above. The Draft / Review Required banner and all existing blocked/non-student-facing governance state must remain visible and unchanged on every route, for all topics, at the public URL exactly as in the internal build. See publicDeploymentAuthorization and authorizationHistory below.

scope.notAuthorized: As of v1.0.0/v1.1.0, all ten non-pilot Chapter 1 topics (ch01-t01, ch01-t04, ch01-t05, ch01-t06, ch01-t07, ch01-t09, ch01-t11, ch01-t12, ch01-t13, ch01-t14) were NOT authorized for any canonical generation under this record, and were NOT within the applicationBuildAuthorization scope added in 1.1.0. As of v1.2.0, this remains true for eight of those ten topics (ch01-t05, ch01-t06, ch01-t07, ch01-t09, ch01-t11, ch01-t12, ch01-t13, ch01-t14) without change. ch01-t01 and ch01-t04 now additionally have a separate, much narrower English-drafting-only authorization — see batch1DraftingAuthorization below — which does NOT place them within authorizedTopicIds above or within applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds; the four-topic scope of both of those has not expanded at any version of this record.

authorizes: Draft-form canonical generation only (instructorScript, contentBlock, problem records) for the four scoped topics
Use of approved scientific corrections (ch01-corr-006, ch01-corr-007, ch01-corr-008) and approved glossary terms (ch01-term-period, ch01-term-frequency) as authoring authority
Newly authored, original explanations, examples, problems, review questions, and diagrams (described as visual specifications), per PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json
doesNotAuthorize: Student-facing publication or display of any generated content (studentFacingAllowed and studentPublicationAuthorized remain false everywhere)
Canonical generation for any topic outside the four listed

Application build, packaging, or deployment beyond the scoped local internal MVP explicitly authorized under this record's v1.1.0 applicationBuildAuthorization (apps/chapter1-mvp/ only) — public deployment, external hosting, student release, and production packaging or distribution remain unauthorized at every version of this record, including v1.1.0
Automatic approval of the generated draft content itself — every generated record is marked draft/review-required and remains blocked pending human scientific and editorial review

Any action listed under prohibitedActions in the v1.1.0 applicationBuildAuthorization section below (authentication, backend or database services, analytics, cloud synchronization, Git staging/commit/push, and modification of any instructional content, baseline, visual, validation, or governance file)

allowedActions: Create an application under apps/chapter1-mvp/
Create React, Vite, and TypeScript source files
Create package and configuration files inside that application directory
Install only dependencies justified by docs/app/MVP_PRODUCT_SPEC.md
Create a package-lock or equivalent dependency lockfile
Run a local development server
Perform local builds
Run local automated and manual tests
Read the four approved pilot JSON files and SVG assets
Create a read-only content adapter
Render the blocked content in an internal-review environment
Create internal-only UI components and routes
Create internal QA records and implementation documentation
prohibitedActions: Public deployment
External hosting
Student release
Student publication
Production packaging or distribution
Authentication
Backend services
Databases
Analytics
Cloud synchronization
Modification of instructional content
Modification of Arabic or English baselines
Modification of source SVG assets
Changing studentFacingAllowed to true
Changing blocked content to approved
Chapter-wide content generation
Topics outside the four-topic pilot
Staging, committing, or pushing through Git
governanceClarifications: Application build authorization is not publication authorization.
Local internal rendering does not change the governance status of any content record.
All four topics and visuals remain draft/review-required.
The Draft / Review Required banner must remain visible in the application at all times.
Content and governance files must be treated as read-only by the application.

Any content correction must follow the existing baseline revision-control process (a baselineVersion bump plus a revisionLog entry in ENGLISH_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json / ARABIC_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json), never a silent application-side edit.
applicationBuildAuthorization.relationToOriginalAuthorization: Authorization v1.0.0 (granted 2026-07-15) explicitly did not authorize application build, packaging, or deployment — see authorizationHistory below for the preserved original doesNotAuthorize wording. Version 1.1.0 does not retract or reinterpret that historical fact; it adds a new, narrowly scoped authorization on top of it, limited to a local, internal Chapter 1 MVP application for the same four already-authorized topics, with publication and deployment remaining explicitly unauthorized.

prerequisiteCorrectionStatus.ch01-corr-002: editoriallyApproved, with a recorded qualification — see SCIENTIFIC_CORRECTIONS.json ch01-corr-002.approvals.approvalBasis: 'apparent weight' is scientifically valid, project-authored interpretive terminology and must not be represented as verbatim source wording.

batch1DraftingAuthorization.scopeNote: This authorization is English-language canonical DRAFT generation only, for exactly these two topics. It is separate from, additive to, and does not modify scope.authorizedTopicIds above or applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds, both of which remain exactly ch01-t02/ch01-t03/ch01-t08/ch01-t10.
allowedActions: Inspect all audited source evidence for ch01-t01 and ch01-t04
Create English instructorScript records for ch01-t01 and ch01-t04, per CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json schemaVersion 2.3.0
Create English learner-visible contentBlock records for ch01-t01 and ch01-t04
Create English instructor-only contentBlock records where justified
Create original, rights-safe worked examples
Create original, rights-safe problem records and solutions
Preserve complete source and scientific-audit lineage via provenanceLinks / sourceReferenceIds, without reproducing copyrighted source text
Apply ch01-corr-001 exactly as approved to ch01-t01 drafting
Apply ch01-corr-002 exactly as approved, including its recorded qualification, to ch01-t04 drafting

Represent Arabic content using CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json's own existing mechanism for untranslated content (arabicSeparation.translationStatus: "missing"; localizedText.text: null and localizedText.status: "missing" on both originalArabicText and canonicalArabicTranslation) — never a fabricated placeholder and never English text copied into an Arabic field
Create validation records for the generated draft files, following the existing pilot visual-validation naming and structure convention
Create textual visual-specification / visual-requirement records as planning metadata only
Run JSON/schema/content validation against CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json schemaVersion 2.3.0 and against IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json's namespace patterns
Create draft-review documentation

Runtime or machine translation of any kind

Claiming independent-expert (physics) review or approval

Chapter-wide generation
Any change to CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json's schemaVersion
Modification of docs/content-audits/ or any audited source evidence

Close paraphrase of publisher wording; copying source examples with superficial number changes; copying review-card or Kahoot wording; reproducing publisher tables; tracing or reusing publisher figures; presenting source artwork as original project work
Git staging, commit, or push

authorizedOutputPaths.primary: docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json and docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t04-content.json — a new directory, not yet created by this authorization task itself.
authorizedOutputPaths.validationRecords: docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ (co-located *-validation.json files, following the existing pilot visual-validation naming convention).
authorizedOutputPaths.rationale: A new, clearly separate directory rather than docs/content-design/chapter-01/pilot/, because "pilot/" is already a governance-defined term scoped exactly to the four build-authorized topics (scope.authorizedTopicIds above). Batch 1 drafts are English-only, not build-authorized, and not baseline-approved; keeping them physically separate prevents any future ambiguity about which topics are actually build-authorized for apps/chapter1-mvp/, even though each drafted file would itself be schema-valid against CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json if it were ever placed there.

rightsRestrictions: All student-facing explanations, examples, questions, and solutions must be newly authored — the same substantive originality commitment already made in PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json for the four pilot topics.

PILOT_RIGHTS_POLICY.json's own scope.appliesToTopicIds does not currently include ch01-t01 or ch01-t04, and this authorization does not amend that file; the same originality requirement is restated here directly for Batch 1 rather than relying on an unamended policy file's scope.
validationRequirements: Every generated draft record must validate against CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json schemaVersion 2.3.0.
Every source/audit citation must resolve against IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json's namespace patterns and authority files.
ch01-corr-001's and ch01-corr-002's corrected wording, and ch01-corr-002's recorded qualification, must be applied exactly as they appear in SCIENTIFIC_CORRECTIONS.json, without alteration.

Any new scientific ambiguity or conflict discovered during drafting must stop work on the affected topic and be documented for a separate review — it must not be silently resolved as an implicit new correction.
publicationRestrictions: studentFacingAllowed and studentPublicationAuthorized remain false for ch01-t01 and ch01-t04, chapter-wide and per-record, exactly as for all fourteen Chapter 1 topics.

Draft records produced under this authorization are not an approved English baseline — a separate baseline-approval action, mirroring ENGLISH_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json's existing pattern, is required before any further step.
This authorization does not move ch01-t01 or ch01-t04 into applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds above, which remains exactly the original four pilot topics.
batch1DraftingAuthorization.relationToOriginalAuthorization: This is an additive amendment, parallel in kind to how v1.1.0 added applicationBuildAuthorization on top of v1.0.0. It does not retract, reinterpret, or expand scope.authorizedTopicIds or applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds, both of which remain exactly ch01-t02/ch01-t03/ch01-t08/ch01-t10. Content drafting authorized here is entirely separate from, and does not by itself authorize, any application implementation work for ch01-t01 or ch01-t04.

batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization.correctionNote: This section was corrected in 1.3.1 before any Arabic content was generated under it. Three defects in the 1.3.0 text were fixed here: (1) an in-place-editing packaging model was replaced with a separate immutable-original / candidate-copy model; (2) 'Arabic canonical generation' terminology was replaced with 'Arabic candidate-draft generation' throughout; (3) an absolute prohibition on any terminology outside the nine Batch 1 glossary records was replaced with a rule enforcing those nine where applicable while permitting ordinary Arabic language and other already-approved glossary terms. See docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_AUTHORIZATION_CORRECTION_RECORD.md for the full correction record, and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_GENERATION_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md's own dated correction section for the additively-preserved original text and what superseded it.

projectOwnerRatification.decision: The project owner explicitly authorizes controlled Arabic candidate-draft generation for ch01-t01 and ch01-t04 under the corrected authorization model recorded at authorizationVersion 1.3.1 (this batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization section), PILOT_READINESS.json pilotReadinessVersion 1.8.1, and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_AUTHORIZATION_CORRECTION_RECORD.md.
projectOwnerRatification.correctedAuthorizationDependency: authorizationVersion 1.3.1 (batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization, as corrected); technical scope, packaging model, output paths, glossary-enforcement rule, and restrictions are unchanged by this ratification

englishBaselineDependency.verifiedAtRatification: 2026-07-17 — both checksums re-computed and confirmed identical to ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json's approvedDraftFiles values immediately before this ratification. No drift.

projectOwnerRatification.glossaryDependency: BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json, glossaryVersion 1.3.0, all nine Batch 1 glossary actions approved (unchanged)
projectOwnerRatification.immutableEnglishBaselineRestriction: The approved English baseline files at docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ (ch01-t01-content.json, ch01-t04-content.json) remain immutable and must never be edited by the Arabic candidate-draft generation task, in place or otherwise.
ratificationDoesNotAuthorize: Modification of the approved English baseline files
Arabic baseline approval (remains unapproved; a separate future ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json-style action is required)
Canonical promotion of any Arabic candidate content
Any claim of independent human scientific review (which remains incomplete)
projectOwnerRatification.governanceHistoryClarification: Recorded transparently, not silently rewritten: (a) authorizationVersion 1.3.0 was created before the project owner had issued the intended explicit authorization decision; (b) authorizationVersion 1.3.1 technically corrected 1.3.0's packaging, terminology, and glossary-enforcement defects but was a correction task, not the project-owner ratification itself; (c) this 1.3.2 ratification supplies the explicit project-owner authorization decision for the already-corrected scope; (d) no Arabic content was generated before this ratification; (e) no Arabic candidate directory (docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-arabic-drafts/) existed before this ratification.
projectOwnerRatification.ratificationBasis: Explicit project-owner decision (khubrani2a-gif), formalized in docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_GENERATION_RATIFICATION_RECORD.md. This is a project-owner governance decision, not an independent human scientific or linguistic review, which remains incomplete.

prerequisites.englishBaselineApproved: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json, baselineVersion 1.0.0, status approved, unchanged since approval

englishBaselineChecksums.verifiedResult: Both checksums re-computed and confirmed identical to ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json's approvedDraftFiles values, both during the original 1.3.0 grant and again during this 1.3.1 correction. No drift.

prerequisites.glossaryReconciliationComplete: BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json, glossaryVersion 1.3.0 — all nine Batch 1 glossary actions (two new terms created and approved: ch01-term-fundamental-quantity, ch01-term-derived-quantity; four pending terms approved: ch01-term-speed, ch01-term-scalar, ch01-term-mass, ch01-term-weight; one term approved with revision: ch01-term-apparent-weight; two existing terms topic-extended: ch01-term-distance, ch01-term-acceleration) are complete, per PHSH111_BATCH1_GLOSSARY_APPROVAL_RECORD.md

batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization.scopeNote: This authorization is controlled Arabic candidate-draft generation only, for exactly these two already English-baseline-approved topics. Arabic candidate generation is authorized; Arabic canonical or baseline approval is not. It is separate from, additive to, and does not modify scope.authorizedTopicIds, applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds, or batch1DraftingAuthorization.applicableTopicIds above, all of which remain unchanged.

packagingModel.principle: The approved English baseline files are immutable and must never be edited by the Arabic-generation task. Arabic candidate drafts are separate new copies, not in-place edits.
packagingModel.overlayModelConsidered: An overlay model (an Arabic-only file referencing the English file by ID, without duplicating English content) was considered and not authorized. schemaVersion 2.3.0's contentBlock/instructorScript/problem records are self-contained objects with English and Arabic fields co-located on the same record (per CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json's $defs and the four pilot topics' own precedent) — nothing in the schema demonstrably prevents a full structural copy with only the arabic sub-object populated, so the separate-full-copy model is used instead, consistent with this task's instruction to prefer it unless an overlay is demonstrably required.
packagingModel.copyingMechanism: A structural deep copy of the approved English JSON structure (topicId, topicTitle, generationStatus/generationNote restated for the candidate file, and the full records array in the same order), preserving every field byte-for-byte except the arabic sub-object on each record, which is populated in the copy only. This is the same structural-fidelity standard already implied by CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json's own schema validation (a record either matches the schema and its sibling English content, or it does not) — no new copying mechanism or schema field is introduced.

allowedActions: Verify, before any generation work, that both approved English baseline files' checksums still match englishBaselineChecksums above; stop and report if either does not match
Create the new directory docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-arabic-drafts/

Create docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-arabic-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json and ch01-t04-content.json as new candidate files, each beginning from the exact approved English baseline structure of its docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ counterpart

In each candidate file, preserve every English field byte-for-byte (all localizedContent.en / problemStatement.en / etc. text, unit values, symbols), every record ID (instructorScriptId, blockId, problemId), record order, and every source/audit/correction/visibility/governance field (provenanceLinks, sourceTraceability, scientificCorrectionIds, conflictRecordIds, duplicateHandling, blocking, contentLeakTestStatus, visualReferenceIds, visualGovernance) exactly as they appear in the approved English baseline file

Populate only the authorized Arabic localization structures in the candidate copies: each contentBlock record's arabic.originalArabicText.text and arabic.canonicalArabicTranslation.text, moving arabic.translationStatus from "missing" to "draft" (the same schema-valid, pilot-precedented value used across all four already-produced pilot topics' own Arabic content; never directly to "approved")

Add an equivalent arabic field (originalArabicText / canonicalArabicTranslation / translationStatus: "draft" / translationReviewer: null / terminologyApprovalStatus / glossaryTermIds) to instructorScript and problem records in the candidate copies only, in the same shape already established for contentBlock records
Populate each candidate record's arabic.glossaryTermIds with the exact approved BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json termId values actually used in that record's Arabic text

Generate Arabic text for every applicable record following the per-record-type strategy already documented in PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_READINESS_GLOSSARY_DECISION_BRIEF.md (direct translation with glossary enforcement for instructorScript, mainIdea, organizedExplanation, misconception, and reviewQuestion content; conceptual adaptation of prose combined with exact preservation of mathematical notation for equationSet and numberedSolution content; a natural Arabic instructional rewrite, not a literal translation, for problem.problemStatement)

Enforce the nine Batch 1 glossary decisions wherever their specific concept appears (see glossaryEnforcement below), while also drawing on any other already-approved BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json term where its concept is relevant, using ordinary Arabic language for anything that is not itself a scientific term needing glossary control, and leaving mathematical symbols/SI units untranslated

Apply the equation-isolation and bidi handling already confirmed sufficient in PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_READINESS_GLOSSARY_DECISION_BRIEF.md §13/§14 (LTR-isolated spans, italic Latin variables, upright SI units, true superscript exponents, plain-ASCII × multiplication) without inventing any new rendering mechanism

Preserve ch01-t04's explicit English qualification that "apparent weight" is project-authored interpretive terminology, not verbatim source wording, as an explicit Arabic sentence rather than dropping it

After generation, verify the two original docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ files remain byte-identical to englishBaselineChecksums above, and verify the English portions inside the new candidate copies match the approved baseline exactly
Create validation records for the two candidate files, following the existing pilot visual-validation naming convention, co-located in docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-arabic-drafts/
Run JSON/schema validation against CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json schemaVersion 2.3.0 on the candidate files after every edit
Create Arabic candidate-draft review documentation

glossaryEnforcement.rule: The nine Batch 1 glossary decisions are mandatory wherever their specific concept appears. All other glossary terms already approved in BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json may also be used wherever their concept is relevant. Ordinary Arabic language does not require glossary registration. Mathematical symbols and SI units remain untranslated. A new controlled scientific term may not be introduced as canonical terminology without a separate glossary decision. An unregistered ordinary explanatory phrase is permitted when it does not function as a new canonical scientific term.
glossaryEnforcement.otherApprovedTermsMayBeUsed: Any other BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json term with approvalStatus: "approved" (e.g. ch01-term-velocity, ch01-term-free-fall, ch01-term-centripetal-acceleration, ch01-term-period, ch01-term-frequency) may be used if its concept is genuinely relevant to a Batch 1 record's Arabic text; none is currently expected to be needed given the two topics' approved English content, but none is prohibited either.
glossaryEnforcement.ordinaryLanguageException: Ordinary, unambiguous Arabic vocabulary with no competing or confusable scientific term (e.g. "time"/الوقت أو الزمن, as already resolved for ch01-t01 in PHSH111_BATCH1_GLOSSARY_INVENTORY_RECONCILIATION_ADDENDUM.md §4/§7) does not require its own glossary entry and is permitted without registration.
glossaryEnforcement.newTermProhibition: A future generation task may not coin a new controlled scientific term (i.e. present a novel Arabic phrase as if it were the project's official, canonical rendering of a physics concept) without a separate glossary decision, following the existing create/approve pattern already used for the nine Batch 1 terms. It may use an unregistered ordinary explanatory phrase or paraphrase precisely because that phrase is not functioning as new canonical terminology — the distinction is between coining official vocabulary and writing ordinary explanatory prose.

apparentWeightPreservation.primaryTerm: الوزن الظاهري (ch01-term-apparent-weight, mandatory wherever apparent weight is discussed)
apparentWeightPreservation.supportingPhrase: قراءة الميزان may be used as a supporting explanatory phrase, exactly as already recorded as an alternateArabicTerm in BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json
apparentWeightPreservation.discouraged: الوزن الحقيقي remains discouraged and must not be used as the preferred contrast term for apparent weight, per BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json's own discouragedTranslations entry and usageNotes.

glossaryEnforcement.discouragedTranslationsRule: discouragedTranslations recorded on any of the nine mandatory terms (or on any other approved term actually used) must not be used as a substitute for that term's approvedArabicTerm.

prohibitedActions: Any modification whatsoever of docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json or ch01-t04-content.json — these approved English baseline files must remain immutable and are never edited, in place or otherwise, by this authorization
Arabic baseline approval (an ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json-style record is a separate, later, project-owner action, mirroring ARABIC_PILOT_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json's existing pattern)

Treating any Arabic candidate-draft file or record as canonical, approved, or promoted — candidate files remain draft, blocked, non-student-facing, not Arabic-baseline-approved, not application-authorized, and not publication-authorized until a separate future approval
Setting any record's arabic.translationStatus directly to "approved" or its terminologyApprovalStatus directly to "approved"
Any modification of ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json, its approvedDraftChecksumSha256 values, or its approval status
Creating, approving, or modifying any BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json term

Coining a new controlled scientific term as if it were approved canonical terminology without a separate glossary decision (see glossaryEnforcement.newTermProhibition above) — this is narrower than, and replaces, 1.3.0's blanket prohibition on any terminology outside the nine Batch 1 terms
Visual (SVG or raster) production of any kind, or translation of visualReference specification text into Arabic beyond what already exists as internal planning metadata
Application-route or topic-registry expansion; rendering ch01-t01 or ch01-t04, or their Arabic candidate files, inside apps/chapter1-mvp/
Public deployment, student release, or any student-facing publication
Claiming independent human expert (linguistic or scientific) review or approval
Arabic or English canonical generation for any topic outside ch01-t01 and ch01-t04
Chapter-wide generation
Any change to CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json's schemaVersion
Modification of docs/content-audits/ or any audited source evidence
Machine translation of any kind in place of authored, glossary-enforced Arabic text
Git staging, commit, or push

authorizedOutputPaths.primary: docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-arabic-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json and docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-arabic-drafts/ch01-t04-content.json — new candidate files in a new, separate directory, not yet created by this authorization task itself. docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ (the approved English baseline) is explicitly NOT an authorized output path for any edit under this authorization.
authorizedOutputPaths.validationRecords: docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-arabic-drafts/ (co-located *-validation.json files, following the existing pilot visual-validation naming convention).
authorizedOutputPaths.rationale: Separate from docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ (so the approved, checksum-verified English baseline can never be silently altered by Arabic-generation work), separate from docs/content-design/chapter-01/pilot/ (already scoped exactly to the four build-authorized topics), and outside apps/chapter1-mvp/ (no application integration is authorized). This corrects 1.3.0's in-place-editing model, which risked silently mutating an approved, checksum-referenced baseline file.

validationRequirements: Verify both approved English baseline files' checksums match englishBaselineChecksums above before any generation work begins.
Every candidate-file record must remain valid against CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json schemaVersion 2.3.0 after the Arabic fields are populated.

Every mandatory-term concept (glossaryEnforcement.mandatoryTermIds) must use its approvedArabicTerm.text exactly where that concept appears; discouragedTranslations must not be used as a substitute for any term actually used.
arabic.translationStatus must read "draft" (never "approved") on every newly populated candidate record until a separate Arabic baseline approval occurs.

After generation, re-checksum docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json and ch01-t04-content.json and confirm both are still byte-identical to englishBaselineChecksums above — fail the task if either has changed.

Verify the English portions inside each Arabic candidate copy (all English text, record IDs, record order, and source/audit/correction/visibility/governance fields) match the approved baseline exactly.

Any new scientific or terminological ambiguity discovered while generating Arabic text must stop work on the affected record and be documented for a separate review — never silently resolved as an implicit new correction or an ad hoc canonical term.

batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization.relationToOriginalAuthorization: This is a corrective patch to the 1.3.0 grant, parallel in kind to how v1.1.0 and v1.2.0 each added a new, additive authorization section — except that 1.3.1 corrects defects within an already-added section rather than adding a new one. It does not retract, reinterpret, or expand scope.authorizedTopicIds, applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds, or batch1DraftingAuthorization.applicableTopicIds above, all of which remain unchanged. prerequisites.baselineChecksumsVerifiedAt: 2026-07-17 — all six approved files (2 English, 2 Arabic, 2 SVG) re-checksummed and confirmed byte-identical to their respective approval records immediately before this authorization

batch1ApplicationIntegrationAuthorizatio.scopeNote: This authorization is INTERNAL APPLICATION INTEGRATION AND QA only, for exactly the two named topics. It does not authorize student-facing release, public deployment, or publication, and it does not itself perform any integration — it authorizes a future, separate integration task. It is separate from, additive to, and does not modify scope.authorizedTopicIds or applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds above, both of which remain exactly the original four pilot topics.

selectedIntegrationSourceModel.rationale: Mirrors the exact pattern already proven by src/content/rawImports.ts for the four pilot topics: live ES-module imports of the canonical files themselves (via Vite/Rollup's module graph), never a copied or duplicated source of truth. vite.config.ts's existing server.fs.allow already resolves to the parent docs/content-design/chapter-01/ directory (not narrowly to pilot/), so it already covers batch1-drafts/, batch1-arabic-drafts/, and batch1-visuals/ without requiring any config change. Because Batch 1's approved baseline is split across two separate immutable files per topic (English-only and a separately-approved Arabic candidate copy), unlike the four pilot topics' single already-merged file each, the future integration task must add a read-only, in-memory merge step (in the content-loading layer, never writing a merged file to disk) that combines each approved English record with its corresponding approved Arabic record's arabic.canonicalArabicTranslation text, producing the same single-file-shaped structure the existing adapter already expects — without duplicating, copying, or creating any new editable source file.
rejectedAlternatives: App-local immutable integration copies — rejected: would create a second, physically separate copy of already-approved content, reintroducing exactly the duplicate-source-of-truth risk this project's governance has consistently avoided at every prior stage (English/Arabic baseline separation, visual asset immutability).

A generated manifest/adapter layer that pre-serializes a merged file to disk — rejected for the same reason: any generated artifact becomes a second source that could drift from the approved originals without being caught.

integrityRequirements: Approved baseline files remain immutable — the integration task must never edit any of the six authorized source paths above.
No duplicate editable source of truth — no app-local copy, no generated merged file written to disk; any merge must occur in memory at load time.

Imported content must remain checksum-verifiable — the integration task must add a load-time or test-time checksum check comparing each imported file's bytes against its approval-record checksum, failing loudly (an AdapterDiagnostic error, matching the existing diagnostics pattern in src/content/validate.ts) rather than silently, if any approved file has drifted.
No silent fallback to unapproved content on a missing or invalid file — matching the existing validate.ts convention of diagnostics-over-silent-omission.
No runtime machine translation of any kind.
No remote content fetching — all sources remain local repository files.
allowedActions: Add ch01-t01 and ch01-t04 to the application's topic registry (src/types/pilotSchema.ts's topic-ID union and topic-order constant, and src/config/topics.ts's re-export), in correct chapter-wide numerical order alongside the existing four pilot topics
Add internal routes and navigation entries for both topics, using the existing generic dynamic route (chapter/1/topic/:topicId) — no new route pattern is required

Load the approved English and Arabic topic records via direct import from the six authorized source paths above, merging English and Arabic content in memory at load time as described in selectedIntegrationSourceModel
Load the approved SVG assets via direct import from the two authorized visual paths above

Render all authorized record types (instructorScript, contentBlock — mainIdea, organizedExplanation, equationSet, visualReference, misconception, reviewQuestion — and problem) through the existing adapter, extending it only as narrowly as required (see requiredAdapterChanges below)
Render instructor-only records (visibility: instructor) only in the existing instructor-review panel, never in the learner-facing flow
Render reviewQuestion content using the existing application-facing label "Review Card" / "بطاقة مراجعة" — the canonical schema blockType reviewQuestion itself must not be renamed in code or registry

Render ch01-prob-104 and its numbered solution through the existing ProblemCard/SolutionReveal components; render ch01-t01 (which has no problem record) without an empty problem section, exactly as the existing adapter's optional problem field already supports
Support English/Arabic switching for both topics via the existing LanguageContext/LanguageToggle
Preserve RTL/LTR switching and equation isolation for both topics' content
Provide enlarge, zoom, and pan access for both visuals via the existing VisualViewer dialog component
Add or update application tests covering both topics (see testingAuthorization below)

Make narrowly required Vite/TypeScript configuration changes strictly limited to what §selectedIntegrationSourceModel and requiredAdapterChanges identify as necessary — no unrelated configuration change
Run TypeScript checks, unit/integration tests, a production build, and internal browser-based QA as part of the integration task itself
requiredAdapterChanges: Extend the PilotTopicId union type (src/types/pilotSchema.ts) from the current four literals ("ch01-t02"|"ch01-t03"|"ch01-t08"|"ch01-t10") to include "ch01-t01" and "ch01-t04".

Extend or add a chapter-wide topic-order constant reflecting the correct numerical sequence — ch01-t01, ch01-t02, ch01-t03, ch01-t04, ch01-t08, ch01-t10 — while preserving PILOT_TOPIC_ORDER's existing four-topic meaning for any logic that is specifically pilot-scoped (e.g. the four-topic exact-count assertion in validateTopicSet), since PILOT_TOPIC_ORDER mirrors the chapter-wide-distinct four-topic pilot authorization, not Batch 1's separate authorization.

Update src/content/rawImports.ts with new import statements for the two approved English files, the two approved Arabic files, the two approved SVGs, and the two approved visual-validation JSON files, following the exact existing import pattern — plus the new in-memory English/Arabic merge step required by selectedIntegrationSourceModel, since Batch 1's baseline (unlike the four pilot topics) is split across two separate approved files per topic.

Update src/content/validate.ts's validateTopicSet topic-count/order assertions to reflect the new six-topic chapter-wide set, without weakening its existing per-record duplicate-ID, schema-version, or governance-field checks.
Update ChapterOnePage.tsx's hardcoded introductory text (currently states 'Four pilot topics are available...') to accurately describe six topics, without altering its governance meaning.

No change is required to ContentBlockType, VisibilityState, BlockingState, or any other structural type — Batch 1 uses only blockTypes already present in KNOWN_BLOCK_TYPES (mainIdea, organizedExplanation, equationSet, visualReference, misconception, reviewQuestion; Batch 1 has no 'example' block, which the adapter already treats as optional).
No change is required to VisualValidationRecord's type shape — both Batch 1 visual-validation JSON files were independently confirmed to already contain every field that type requires.

These are the only adapter/configuration changes identified as required by this authorization's own architecture review; the integration task itself must independently re-verify this list before implementation and document any further narrowly-scoped change it discovers as necessary, following this same additive, non-silent pattern.
prohibitedActions: Any modification of the approved English or Arabic Batch 1 baseline files, the approved SVGs, or either visual-validation record
Any modification of ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json, ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json, VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json, or IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json
Creating any app-local copy of approved content or visual assets, or any generated file that duplicates or could drift from an approved source
Student-facing release, public deployment, or publication of any kind

Unrelated application redesign, refactor, or dependency changes beyond what requiredAdapterChanges identifies

Runtime machine translation
Remote content fetching

reviewBasis: Explicit project-owner instruction given directly in chat on 2026-07-17: "I authorize publishing the PHSH111 application publicly through GitHub Pages from main."
publicDeploymentAuthorization.scopeNote: This authorization is PUBLIC HOSTING of the built apps/chapter1-mvp/ application via GitHub Pages only. It authorizes the deployment mechanism (a GitHub Actions workflow publishing the Vite production build to GitHub Pages, triggered on push to main), not any change to content, governance flags, or application behavior. It does not move any topic's studentFacingAllowed or studentPublicationAuthorized to true, and it does not itself constitute the kind of pedagogical 'student release' or 'publication' referred to elsewhere in this record's prohibitedActions lists. It is separate from, additive to, and does not modify scope.authorizedTopicIds, applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds, batch1DraftingAuthorization.applicableTopicIds, batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization.applicableTopicIds, batch1VisualProductionAuthorization.applicableTopicIds, or batch1ApplicationIntegrationAuthorization.applicableTopicIds above, all of which remain unchanged.

Run the existing test suite and a production build as a required step of the workflow and before committing, exactly as already authorized under testingAuthorization/allowedActions in prior sections

Unlike v1.1.0 through v1.5.0, which each expanded what could be built or integrated while leaving deployment unauthorized, this amendment authorizes deployment (hosting) of what has already been built and integrated, without touching content or governance-flag scope in any way.

authorizationHistory.summary: Original grant: draft-form canonical content generation only, for ch01-t02/ch01-t03/ch01-t08/ch01-t10. Explicitly did not authorize application build, packaging, or deployment.

authorizationHistory.summary: Controlled amendment adding a scoped authorization to build a local, internal-only Chapter 1 MVP application (apps/chapter1-mvp/) for the same four topics. Content-generation scope is unchanged, no topic was added, and every publication restriction from v1.0.0 remains in force. See applicationBuildAuthorization above and docs/app/MVP_IMPLEMENTATION_DECISIONS.json for the binding implementation decisions made on this basis.

Controlled amendment adding a narrowly scoped English-language canonical DRAFTING authorization (not baseline approval, not application build, not publication) for exactly two additional topics: ch01-t01 and ch01-t04 (Batch 1), following completion of their scientific-reconciliation prerequisite (ch01-corr-001 editoriallyApproved; ch01-corr-002 editoriallyApproved with a recorded qualification on 'apparent weight' terminology). The original four-topic scope.authorizedTopicIds and the v1.1.0 applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds are both unchanged. Arabic content, visual production, application expansion, English baseline approval, and student-facing publication all remain unauthorized for ch01-t01 and ch01-t04. See batch1DraftingAuthorization above and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_DRAFTING_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md for full detail.

Controlled amendment adding a narrowly scoped Arabic-language canonical GENERATION authorization (not Arabic baseline approval, not visual production, not application expansion, not publication) for the same two Batch 1 topics: ch01-t01 and ch01-t04, following completion of the approved English baseline (ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.0) and of all nine Batch 1 glossary actions (BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json v1.3.0). scope.authorizedTopicIds, applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds, and batch1DraftingAuthorization.applicableTopicIds are all unchanged. Arabic baseline approval, visual production, application expansion, and student-facing publication all remain unauthorized for ch01-t01 and ch01-t04. See batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization above and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_GENERATION_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md for full detail.

Governance-repair correction to the 1.3.0 batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization section, applied before any Arabic content was generated under it. Three defects were corrected: (1) the packaging model changed from in-place editing of the approved English baseline files to a separate, immutable-original/candidate-copy model — the only authorized Arabic candidate output paths are now docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-arabic-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json and ch01-t04-content.json, and docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ is now explicitly listed as prohibited to modify; (2) 'Arabic canonical generation' terminology was replaced throughout with 'Arabic candidate-draft generation,' and the record now states explicitly that Arabic candidate generation is authorized while Arabic canonical or baseline approval is not; (3) the absolute prohibition on any terminology outside the nine Batch 1 glossary records was replaced with a rule making those nine mandatory where applicable while permitting other already-approved glossary terms, ordinary unregistered Arabic language, and unregistered explanatory phrases that do not function as new canonical scientific terms. This is a corrective patch, not a new grant of scope — applicableTopicIds remains exactly ch01-t01/ch01-t04, and every restriction already in force (no Arabic baseline approval, no visual production, no application expansion, no publication) remains in force. The superseded 1.3.0 defects are preserved as historical record in the 1.3.0 entry above, in docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_GENERATION_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md's own dated correction section, and in the dedicated docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_AUTHORIZATION_CORRECTION_RECORD.md.
authorizationHistory.reviewBasis: Governance-repair task, formalized in docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_AUTHORIZATION_CORRECTION_RECORD.md and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_GENERATION_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md's added correction section

authorizationHistory.summary: Explicit project-owner ratification of the corrected 1.3.1 batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization scope. Governance history recorded transparently: 1.3.0 was created before the project owner had issued the intended explicit authorization decision; 1.3.1 technically corrected 1.3.0's packaging-model, terminology, and glossary-enforcement defects but was a correction task, not the explicit project-owner ratification itself; this 1.3.2 entry supplies that explicit project-owner ratification for the already-corrected scope. It changes no technical scope, output path, restriction, or dependency established at 1.3.1: applicableTopicIds remains exactly ch01-t01/ch01-t04; the authorized Arabic candidate output paths remain docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-arabic-drafts/ch01-t01-content.json and ch01-t04-content.json; the approved English baseline files at docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-drafts/ remain immutable; Arabic baseline approval, canonical promotion, visual production, application integration, deployment, and student-facing publication all remain unauthorized; and no independent human scientific review is claimed. No Arabic content was generated, and no docs/content-design/chapter-01/batch1-arabic-drafts/ directory existed, before this ratification. English baseline checksums (ch01-t01 a445f55d...; ch01-t04 c876a6fe...) and glossary version (1.3.0) re-verified unchanged at ratification. See batch1ArabicGenerationAuthorization.projectOwnerRatification above and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_ARABIC_GENERATION_RATIFICATION_RECORD.md for full detail.

Controlled amendment adding a narrowly scoped, original, rights-safe VISUAL PRODUCTION authorization (not application integration, not student-facing authorization, not deployment or publication) for exactly two visual IDs: ch01-t01-visual-001 and ch01-t04-visual-001, following completion of the approved English Batch 1 baseline (ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.0) and the approved Arabic Batch 1 baseline (ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.0). scope.authorizedTopicIds and applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds are both unchanged. Application integration, independent human visual/scientific review, and student-facing publication all remain unauthorized/incomplete for ch01-t01 and ch01-t04. Both visuals must remain draft, blocked, studentFacingAllowed: false, reviewer: null, reviewedAt: null at all times under this authorization. See batch1VisualProductionAuthorization above and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_VISUAL_PRODUCTION_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md for full detail.

Controlled amendment adding a narrowly scoped INTERNAL APPLICATION INTEGRATION authorization (not student-facing release, not public deployment, not publication) for the same two Batch 1 topics — ch01-t01 and ch01-t04 — following completion of the approved English Batch 1 baseline (v1.0.0), the approved Arabic Batch 1 baseline (v1.0.0), the approved Visual Batch 1 baseline (v1.0.0), and registration of all 18 required Batch 1 identifiers (IDENTIFIER_REGISTRY.json v1.3.0). scope.authorizedTopicIds and applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds are both unchanged. The selected integration-source model is direct import from the six approved source paths (English, Arabic, visuals), with an in-memory English/Arabic merge step, mirroring the existing rawImports.ts pattern with no duplicate editable source of truth. This version authorizes a future integration task; it does not itself integrate either topic, modify any application file, or run any build/test/install/deploy command. Independent human review, student-facing release, deployment, and publication all remain unauthorized/incomplete for ch01-t01 and ch01-t04. See batch1ApplicationIntegrationAuthorization above and docs/app/PHSH111_BATCH1_APPLICATION_INTEGRATION_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md for full detail.

Controlled amendment adding a narrowly scoped PUBLIC DEPLOYMENT (hosting) authorization for the built apps/chapter1-mvp/ application via GitHub Pages, using a GitHub Actions workflow triggered on every push to main, at the project owner's explicit request and authorization given directly in chat on 2026-07-17. This authorizes the hosting mechanism only: no content, governance flag, or application behavior is changed. studentFacingAllowed and studentPublicationAuthorized remain false for every topic; the Draft / Review Required banner must remain visible at the public URL exactly as in the internal build. scope.authorizedTopicIds, applicationBuildAuthorization.applicableTopicIds, and every Batch 1 section's applicableTopicIds above are all unchanged. See publicDeploymentAuthorization above and docs/app/PHSH111_PUBLIC_DEPLOYMENT_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md for full detail.
authorizationHistory.reviewBasis: Explicit project-owner decision given directly in chat, formalized in docs/app/PHSH111_PUBLIC_DEPLOYMENT_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md
